response.justification: Maria Rodriguez's background is almost entirely in web and full-stack development using TypeScript, Node.js, and D-lang. Her only professional C++ exposure is a brief ~6-month stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) writing components for a C IDE — far short of the mandatory 5–10 years required. She has no fixed income, fixed income derivatives, or capital markets trading-systems experience; her RIDE Capital role touched investment-adjacent fintech via Node/TypeScript, not financial-product infrastructure. There is no evidence of Windows platform focus, SQL Server or Oracle depth, STL proficiency, Python, Summit, or messaging frameworks like Kafka or MQ. The mandatory requirements for this role are almost entirely unmet.
strengths: Consistent CI/CD and DevOps practice across multiple roles demonstrates solid engineering discipline
Brief C/C++ work at National Instruments shows at least baseline familiarity with the language
RIDE Capital role provides peripheral exposure to a fintech/investment context
concerns: Approximately 6 months of professional C++ experience against a mandatory 5–10 year requirement — a critical gap
No fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking systems domain knowledge at any meaningful level
Primary tech stack is TypeScript/Node.js/web with no SQL Server or Oracle experience cited
